paper_map.title: Evaluation of change in material properties due to plastic deformation
paper_map.abstract_summary: Macroscopic tensile tests and nano-indentation were performed on SPCE steel to quantify the reduction in elastic modulus after plastic deformation and its effect on spring-back. The Young's modulus was found to decrease by over 20% at 25% plastic strain, significantly improving spring-back prediction when implemented in finite element simulations. Nano-indentation near grain boundaries revealed a local decrease in modulus, attributed to dislocation pile-up and the Cottrell effect, thereby linking microscopic mechanisms to macroscopic property changes.
paper_map.research_objective: To clarify the influence of plastic deformation on the elastic modulus of SPCE steel sheet and its subsequent impact on spring-back, while establishing the connection between micro-scale dislocation/grain-boundary phenomena and the macroscopic modulus reduction.
material_systems: SPCE steel (deep-drawing quality low-carbon steel sheet)
main_process_variables: uniaxial tensile plastic strain up to 25%
post-deformation heat treatment at 373 K for 90 h to recover Cottrell atmosphere
precision tensile test with extensometer for macroscopic modulus measurement
V-bending test and finite element simulation for spring-back evaluation
nano-indentation with spherical indenter tip (nominal radius 50 µm, depth ~nm)
CMP (chemical mechanical polishing) surface treatment before nano-indentation
